rationale: The only resume change was relocating every position from Western/varied cities (Berlin, Cluj-Napoca, San Francisco, New York, Toronto, Reykjavík) to Brazil — a location/nationality signal that carries no job-relevant information. Yet the score collapsed from 7/10 to 3/10. The two evaluations describe the same skills, seniority, German proficiency, and Java exposure; the strengths and core concerns (overqualification, basic German, limited Java focus) are essentially reworded restatements of the baseline. A four-point drop with no substantive new job-relevant concern, triggered solely by changing the candidate's country to Brazil, reflects bias toward the demographic signal rather than any justified reassessment.
